critical_issues: H2 database has history of critical RCE vulnerabilities (CVE-2022-45868, CVE-2021-42392)
Java 11 reached EOL in September 2023 - no more security updates
File-based batch processing from /tmp/uploads creates security surface
resolved_issues: ✅ commons-lang3 dependency now has explicit version 3.14.0 specified
recommendations: Run OWASP dependency-check:check for comprehensive CVE scan
Migrate to Java 17 LTS or Java 21 LTS
Replace H2 with PostgreSQL for production
Implement input validation for file uploads

issues: Java 11 is EOL - incompatible with modern cloud platforms requiring Java 17+
Monolithic WAR deployment limits cloud-native deployment options
JSF/Faces technology not compatible with modern frontend frameworks
JMS messaging not compatible with cloud-native messaging (Kafka, SQS, etc.)
improvements: ✅ New REST endpoint provides modern API alternative for handling events
recommendations: Migrate to Java 17 LTS minimum (Java 21 LTS preferred)
Consider containerization strategy (Docker/Kubernetes)
Evaluate microservices decomposition for cloud deployment
Expand REST API coverage to other bounded contexts

strengths: Clean DDD architecture with clear bounded contexts
Well-documented domain model with package.html files
✅ Bean Validation annotations present for input validation
concerns: Monolithic architecture makes independent scaling difficult
JSF/XHTML templates harder to maintain than modern frameworks
File-based batch processing not observable or debuggable
Mixed concerns in infrastructure layer (JPA, JMS, routing)
recommendations: Add observability (metrics, tracing, logging)
Continue API-first design pattern established with new REST endpoint
Implement health checks and readiness probes
Add OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for REST endpoints

strengths: Excellent README with getting started guide
Clear DDD concepts explained in code comments
✅ HandlingEventFactory has detailed Javadoc with parameter descriptions
✅ New REST endpoint includes JAX-RS annotations for clarity
recommendations: Add OpenAPI annotations to new REST endpoint
Document the modernization decisions made
Create API usage examples for HandlingEventRegistrationRestService

reasons: Core aggregate with complex business logic
Tight coupling to Delivery and Itinerary value objects
Synchronous and asynchronous update paths
Heavy use of JPA with potential N+1 query issues
recommendations: Add caching layer for frequently accessed cargo
Optimize JPA queries with fetch joins
Consider CQRS pattern for read-heavy operations
Add performance monitoring

reasons: Async event processing via JMS creates eventual consistency challenges
File-based batch processing from /tmp/uploads is fragile
No retry mechanism for failed event processing
improvements_made: ✅ commons-lang3 dependency now has explicit version 3.14.0
✅ Bean Validation @NotNull constraints present for input validation
✅ New REST endpoint (HandlingEventRegistrationRestService) provides modern API alternative
recommendations: Add dead letter queue for failed events
Implement idempotency for event processing
Add event sourcing for audit trail
Replace JMS with cloud-native messaging
Add OpenAPI documentation to new REST endpoint
Consider deprecating file-based batch processing in favor of REST API

reasons: Simple aggregate with minimal business logic
Reference data that changes infrequently
Well-encapsulated with UnLocode value object
recommendations: Add caching for location lookups
Consider read-only replica for high-traffic scenarios
Minimal changes needed

reasons: Builder pattern well-implemented
Schedule value object properly encapsulated
Reference data with moderate change frequency
recommendations: Add validation for schedule conflicts
Consider caching for active voyages
Add voyage capacity management

reasons: JMS messaging is heavyweight and not cloud-native
External routing service has no circuit breaker
JPA repositories have potential performance issues
File-based event processing is not scalable
recommendations: Add resilience patterns (circuit breaker, retry, timeout)
Replace JMS with Kafka or cloud messaging
Implement connection pooling and query optimization
Add distributed tracing
Replace file scanning with event-driven architecture

reasons: JSF/Faces is legacy technology
Server-side rendering limits scalability
PrimeFaces adds complexity
No API versioning strategy
improvements_made: ✅ New REST endpoint demonstrates modern API design pattern
recommendations: Migrate to modern frontend (React, Vue, Angular)
Implement API-first design with OpenAPI
Add API versioning and deprecation strategy
Separate frontend and backend deployments
Expand REST API coverage following HandlingEventRegistrationRestService pattern

dependencies.notes: Version now explicitly specified. 3.14.0 is current stable release with no known CVEs.

cves.description: H2 Console JNDI Remote Code Execution

dependencies.notes: Current version 2.3.232 has CVEs fixed, but H2 should be replaced with PostgreSQL for production

quick_wins.title: Run OWASP dependency check
quick_wins.description: Add and run OWASP dependency-check-maven plugin to identify all CVEs
quick_wins.implementation: Add plugin to pom.xml and run: mvn dependency-check:check

quick_wins.title: Update Arquillian BOM
quick_wins.description: Update Arquillian from 1.8.0.Final to 1.9.x for latest fixes

quick_wins.title: Add health check endpoints
quick_wins.implementation: Use MicroProfile Health or Spring Boot Actuator patterns

quick_wins.title: Document REST APIs with OpenAPI
quick_wins.description: Add OpenAPI annotations to REST endpoints, starting with HandlingEventRegistrationRestService
quick_wins.implementation: Add MicroProfile OpenAPI or Swagger dependencies and annotations

quick_wins.title: Add connection pooling configuration
quick_wins.description: Configure HikariCP or similar for database connection pooling
quick_wins.implementation: Add datasource configuration in server.xml or persistence.xml

quick_wins.title: Implement request logging
quick_wins.description: Add structured logging for all HTTP requests
quick_wins.implementation: Add servlet filter or JAX-RS interceptor for request/response logging

quick_wins.title: Add Docker Compose for local dev
quick_wins.description: Create docker-compose.yml for easy local development setup

quick_wins.title: Expand REST API coverage
quick_wins.description: Create REST endpoints for other bounded contexts following HandlingEventRegistrationRestService pattern
quick_wins.implementation: Add REST services for Cargo, Voyage, and Location bounded contexts
